title: AgentTeams MCP Server Definition (framework-neutral, opt-in)
description: Inert, host-agnostic definition of a Model Context Protocol server for a domain. Emitted ONLY when MCP host-features are enabled (claude:mcp or bridge:copilot-vscode-to-claude:mcp). Generator-owned; secrets are referenced by name, never inlined. This artifact provisions nothing on its own — credentialed activation (writing a live host config that wires real credentials) is a separate, operator-authorized step (see references/mcp-auto-detection-report.md §5.4). Excluded from drift by construction (never in output_files_map/template_hashes/file_hashes; .json so the .md snapshot suite is unaffected).

artifact_type.description: Top-level discriminator (NOT schema_version) so build-log readers never treat this as a baseline.

server_id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for this server within the project.

domain.description: The capability domain this server serves (e.g., 'data-access', 'ticketing').

description.description: Human-readable summary of what the server exposes.

trust_tier.description: SELF-DECLARED trust level. The generator cannot verify this; it is operator-surfaced advisory metadata. third-party-* values force a DEFER_TO_SECURITY_REVIEW recommendation upstream and require security_review.required=true (see allOf below).

transport.description: MCP transport. When 'http', the auth object is required and should carry a url (see allOf below).

command.description: Launch command for a stdio server (e.g., 'uvx', 'npx', 'python'). Optional; required-by-convention to render a runnable stdio extension (e.g. a Goose recipe extension). NOT a secret. Ignored for http transport.

args.description: Arguments passed to 'command' for a stdio server (e.g., ['mcp-server-postgres']). NOT secrets — credentials are referenced via auth.credential_ref / env vars, never inlined here.

auth.description: Credential reference only — NEVER an inline secret. credential_ref is constrained to identifier/key shapes so a raw connection string or token cannot validate.

mechanism.description: How credentials are supplied at activation. 'none' for unauthenticated local servers.

credential_ref.description: Name of the env var or secret-store key (e.g., 'VK_PG_DSN' or 'store:prod/pg'). Pattern forbids whitespace and a raw 'scheme://user:pass@host' form. The value is resolved at activation, never stored here.

url.description: Endpoint for http transport. Required-by-convention when transport='http'.

tools.description: Tool surface this server exposes. Kept explicit so least-privilege scope and side-effect gating can be reasoned about before activation.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's input (passthrough; structure not constrained here).

side_effects.description: read = no mutation; write = reversible mutation (caution flag); destructive = delete/irreversible (forces DEFER_TO_SECURITY_REVIEW upstream and requires security_review.required=true via allOf below).

resources.description: MCP resource primitives (readable data) exposed by the server. Passthrough; structure not constrained here.

prompts.description: MCP prompt primitives (server-supplied templates). Passthrough; structure not constrained here.

scope.description: Least-privilege binding: agent slugs permitted to use this server. An empty array means no agent is bound (definition only).

progressive_disclosure.description: Tool-loading strategy controlling context cost (report §4.1). 'lazy' = tools discovered on demand; 'eager' = all tool definitions loaded upfront. A large/dynamic tool surface should be 'lazy' or it is an efficiency anti-pattern.

security_review.description: Operator-surfaced advisory metadata (report §6.4). NOT a code-enforced emission block in the current pipeline; authorized_by/authorized_at are filled by the operator-authorization process, not by the generator. The allOf clauses below DO force 'required'=true for destructive/third-party servers, giving the report's hard-gate claim schema backing.

allOf.$comment: transport=http requires an auth object (carrying a url).
Hard gate (report §5.1): third-party trust forces security_review.required=true.
Hard gate (report §5.1): any destructive tool forces security_review.required=true.
